summary:
The presence of isolated lung metastases in nasopharyngeal carcinoma does not preclude benefits from combined modalities; recent studies show improved progression-free and overall survival with concurrent chemoradiotherapy compared to radiation or chemotherapy alone. Real-world data demonstrate significantly longer median survivals—77.1 vs. 32.4 months—with added chemo-radiation therapy.

Final Answer: Yes
The provided summary contains only two sentences as required. It focuses exclusively on clinically relevant factors such as type of treatment (chemo-radiotherapy), patient subgroup (isolated lung metastasis), and critical endpoints like progression-free and overall survival, directly addressing the core issue raised in the query about therapeutic efficacy.
